[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2020-12-15
[TITLE]: Was a Living Triceratops Found in Indonesia? | Snopes.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: In December 2020, a video supposedly showing a living triceratops in Indonesia being taken down from a truck a bed was widely circulated online: This video, which has racked up hundreds of thousands of views, garnered a mixture of comments....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: This video actually features a triceratops puppet at a dinosaur theme park in Indonesia called Mojosemi Forest Park. While we're not exactly certain how this puppet was created, a longer video from this dino-themed amusement park appears to show that this triceratops is operated possibly by two people inside of an elaborate costume: 
 While some may have taken pause when they first saw the triceratops video, other videos from Mojosemi Forest Park clearly show that dinosaurs have not actually been brought back from extinction. 
 Surprisingly, this is not the most unbelievable dinosaur topic that we've covered at Snopes.com. A viral photograph of director Steven Spielberg with one of the animatronic triceratops used in the movie "Jurassic Park" is often circulated on social media along with the claim that it shows a big game hunter with his trophy at the end of an exotic safari.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: December 14, 2020
[TITLE]: Indonesians are loving (and somehow believing) that a ‘triceratops’ has arrived at a recreational park in East Java | Coconuts
[METADATA/INTRO]: A video showing a triceratops (yes, the dinosaur) being unloaded from a truck has become a source of amusement and wonder for Indonesians on social media lately, prompting netizens to question if 2020 can get any weirder. Alhamdulillah piaraan baru...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: A video showing a triceratops (yes, the dinosaur) being unloaded from a truck has become a source of amusement and wonder for Indonesians on social media lately, prompting netizens to question if 2020 can get any weirder. 
 Alhamdulillah piaraan baru udah nyampe 🙏 pic.twitter.com/W4ZYOYRAHa 
 — Achmad Zamzami (@PengendaliiApi) December 14, 2020 
 “Alhamdulillah (praise God) the new pet has arrived,” the tweet above reads. 
 To be clear, it’s an elaborate costume of the herbivorous dinosaur, which is famous for its three horns. The video shows the dinosaur resisting while being pulled with ropes by a number of military officers and animal handlers. The 30-second clip has been viewed nearly 880,000 times on Twitter at the time of publication.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2020-12-21
[TITLE]: Video of a Triceratops found in Indonesia is actually part of a cool dino exhibit - Science
[METADATA/INTRO]: Phew, 2020, you're something else. A recent video that purportedly shows Indonesian park workers unloading a 'real-life' triceratops from a truck just goes to show that 2020 isn't done with its shit yet....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Phew, 2020, you're something else. 
 A recent video that purportedly shows Indonesian park workers unloading a 'real-life' triceratops from a truck just goes to show that 2020 isn't done with its shit yet. 
 Since the video surfaced on December 14, 2020, it has garnered over 1.1 million views on Twitter. 
 Alhamdulillah piaraan baru udah nyampe 🙏 pic.twitter.com/W4ZYOYRAHa — Achmad Zamzami (@PengendaliiApi) December 14, 2020 
 The caption, which reads "Praise God, the new pet has arrived" makes the whole thing even more bizarre. 
 In the video, the triceratops can be seen struggling to free itself from its restraints. Tossing and turning, in a desperate bid to escape.
